name: PeerCert — Skills you own, vouched for by real people
description: Peer-to-peer skill endorsements that are digitally signed, owned by you, and portable across apps.

groupPermissions.description: PeerCert lets people vouch for each other's skills with signed, revocable endorsements they can keep, share privately, or publish for cross-app discovery.

protocolPermissions.description: Show your profile and address endorsements to you (getPublicKey with identityKey).
Search for people by name or skill and show names/photos for endorsers (discoverByAttributes / discoverByIdentityKey).
Publish endorsements to public search and take them off again (lock/unlock identity tokens on the overlay network).
Send endorsements to peers and receive theirs via your MessageBox inbox.

basketAccess.description: Store the revocation token created with each endorsement you issue, so you can later revoke it.

spendingAuthorization.description: Create the small on-chain tokens (1 satoshi each, plus mining fees) that anchor endorsements, public listings, and revocations.

counterpartyPermissions.description: Endorsements are exchanged directly with other people, so these protocols are used with a different counterparty for each peer you vouch for, receive from, or share proofs with.

protocols.description: Sign endorsements you issue and verify the signatures on endorsements you receive.
Encrypt endorsement details for the recipient, unlock your own for display, and create keyrings that reveal selected details to a chosen verifier.
Create and spend the revocation token tied to each endorsement's subject.
Mutually authenticate with the MessageBox server and peers when exchanging endorsements.
